items.title: Tooling/spec updates for agent_api.session bucket

prompt: Work item: uaa-0013 — Tooling/spec updates for agent_api.session bucket.
Goal: introduce agent_api.session.* as a first-class capability bucket (rubric + tooling) so onboarding and audits can bucket session capabilities mechanically.
Steps:
1) Update docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md to include agent_api.session.* in the bucket rubric list (near agent_api.exec.*).
2) Update crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix.rs: bucket_for(...) should recognize agent_api.session.* and bucket_order(...) should include agent_api.session in the canonical bucket ordering.
3) Run the generators/audits: `cargo run -p xtask -- capability-matrix` and `cargo run -p xtask -- capability-matrix-audit`.
Output: PR-ready spec + tooling changes; no backend behavior changes.
deliverables: Updated bucket rubric + xtask bucketing for agent_api.session.*

notes: Update docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md rubric to include agent_api.session.*.
Update crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix.rs bucket_for(...) and bucket_order(...) to recognize agent_api.session.* so the generated matrix groups session capabilities correctly.

Confirm crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix_audit.rs behavior once any agent_api.session.* capabilities are added (audit requires new agent_api.* capabilities to be supported by >=2 built-in backends unless allowlisted).

items.title: Obs facet schema (events + completion)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0014 — Obs facet schema (events + completion).
Goal: define a stable, bounded obs metadata schema that can carry run_id / trace_context / tags across AgentWrapperEvent.data and AgentWrapperCompletion.data.

Key constraint: ToolCall/ToolResult events already use `data.schema = agent_api.tools.structured.v1`; obs metadata must coexist without breaking the tools facet contract (likely via an optional nested `obs` object in `data`, not a competing top-level schema for tool events).
Steps:
1) Specify the obs object shape + strict bounds (identifiers + small maps only; no raw backend lines).
2) Specify where the obs data MAY appear (which event kinds, completion.data) and merge/precedence rules when both caller and backend supply context.
3) Specify how this schema relates to upcoming surfaces: agent_api.obs.run_id.v1, agent_api.obs.trace_context.v1, agent_api.obs.tags.v1.
Output: spec text updates ready for implementation (keep it conservative and backward-compatible with existing tools facet rules).
deliverables: A concrete obs facet schema (v1) + placement rules + bounds, suitable to unblock run_id/trace_context/tags work

notes: Define a single bounded obs facet schema carried in AgentWrapperEvent.data and/or AgentWrapperCompletion.data (AgentWrapperEvent has no top-level run/session/trace fields).
This facet should be the foundation for: agent_api.obs.run_id.v1, agent_api.obs.trace_context.v1, agent_api.obs.tags.v1.
Decide emission model: always emit vs gated by explicit capabilities and/or an extension key; define precedence + merge rules when both backend and caller supply context.

items.title: Research/plan: backend config pass-through (bounded)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0012 — Research/plan: backend config pass-through (bounded).

Goal: do a final sweep of codex + claude_code flags/settings/config surfaces and decide what becomes universal (`agent_api.config.*` or other agent_api.* keys) vs what stays backend-specific (bounded pass-through under backend.<agent_kind>.*).
Steps:
1) Inventory: extract global flags + exec/print flags + key=value config mechanisms from cli_manifests/*/current.json and wrapper_coverage manifests.
2) Normalize by semantics: model selection, context roots, safety/permissions, tool allow/deny, output/artifacts, session selectors, MCP, etc.
3) Promote only backend-neutral semantics into agent_api.*; everything else becomes one or more bounded backend pass-through keys with strict validation/bounds.
Output: a concrete proposed key list (universal keys + backend pass-through keys), including JSON schema sketches, defaults, bounds, and validation rules.
deliverables: Concrete list of proposed universal config keys (agent_api.config.*)
Concrete list of bounded backend pass-through keys (backend.codex.* / backend.claude_code.*) with schema/bounds

notes: Final pass before implementation: inventory codex + claude_code global flags, key=value config surfaces, and settings inputs (via cli_manifests/*/current.json and wrapper coverage manifests).
Promote only backend-neutral semantics into agent_api.config.* (and/or other universal buckets); keep backend-specific knobs under backend.<agent_kind>.* even if they are 'config'.

Produce a concrete list of bounded backend pass-through keys we want to support (e.g. backend.codex.cli_overrides.v1, backend.claude_code.settings.v1), including schema, defaults, bounds, and validation rules.

items.title: Session/thread id surfacing (for resume-by-id UX)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0015 — Session/thread id surfacing (for resume-by-id UX).
Goal: expose the backend’s session/thread identifier to the caller as stable, bounded metadata so resume-by-id is ergonomic.
Inputs already exist via streaming/log parsing:
- Codex: ThreadEvent::ThreadStarted.thread_id (thread.started/thread.resumed).
- Claude: stream-json session_id (SystemInit and other events).
Steps:
1) Decide the universal surface: where does the identifier live (completion.data vs an early Status event data payload vs both) and what is the stable schema/name.
2) Implement extraction + storage in both agent_api backends (Claude: capture session_id from stream-json; Codex: capture thread_id from JSONL ThreadStarted).
3) Gate it behind a capability id (recommended) and ensure it respects bounds + redaction rules.
Output: cross-backend consistent session/thread id surfacing + tests (fake-binary where appropriate).
deliverables: Stable session/thread id payload surfaced to callers (schema + implementation + tests)

notes: Make session/thread identifiers discoverable to callers as a stable, bounded payload (so resume-by-id can be ergonomic).
Leverage existing streaming/log parsing: Codex exposes thread_id in thread.started/thread.resumed events; Claude exposes session_id in stream-json events.
Decide where the identifier lives (e.g. completion.data and/or an initial Status event facet) and whether it is always emitted or gated behind an explicit capability.
Use stream-json parsing to extract session_id (per docs/specs/claude-stream-json-parser-contract.md) and surface it in a stable payload.
Use JSONL parsing to extract ThreadEvent::ThreadStarted.thread_id (thread.started/thread.resumed) and surface it in a stable payload.

items.title: Non-interactive execution policy
notes: Intent: do not hang on interactive permission/approval prompts in headless automation.
Owned by docs/specs/universal-agent-api/extensions-spec.md.

items.title: Universal model selection
notes: Expose model selection behind a core key shared by codex and claude_code.
Define schema/defaults in docs/specs/universal-agent-api/extensions-spec.md, then implement + gate in both backends.

items.title: Universal add-dir / extra context roots
notes: Provide a single cross-agent way to add extra context directories for tool access / indexing / file reads.
Value should be a bounded list of paths; decide absolute vs relative semantics in the core spec.

items.title: Universal resume: selector=last
notes: Resume the most recent session/thread in the working dir (backend-defined persistence store).
Keep agent_api.run prompt non-empty; this is 'resume + send follow-up prompt', not 'resume with no new prompt'.

items.title: Universal resume: selector=id
notes: Resume a specific session/thread by identifier.
Claude uses UUID session ids; Codex accepts UUID or thread name (UUID wins if it parses).

items.title: Universal MCP management commands
notes: Unify a minimal MCP management surface (add/get/list/remove) across backends.
May require a non-run API (not AgentWrapperRunRequest.extensions), since these are CLI subcommands.
Claude also has mcp add-json, add-from-claude-desktop, serve, reset-project-choices (backend-specific).
Codex add supports --url vs -- <COMMAND...>; Claude add uses <commandOrUrl> [args...] with different flag shapes.

items.title: Universal fork session
notes: Expose a cross-agent 'fork conversation' operation when both CLIs support it in headless mode.
Verify Codex fork is available in the minimum supported Codex version for this repo's policy; older Codex builds may have only interactive / slash-command equivalents.

items.title: Obs: stable run_id emission
notes: Expose a stable per-run id for correlation across events + completion.
Contract constraint: AgentWrapperEvent has no run_id field, so this must be expressed via AgentWrapperEvent.data (facet schema) and/or completion.data.
Decide whether run_id is always generated (recommended) vs only present when explicitly requested via an extension key.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would generate and attach metadata.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would generate and attach metadata.

items.title: Obs: trace context propagation
notes: Allow callers to provide trace context that is attached to events and (when possible) propagated to the underlying backend process.
Define a concrete schema (e.g. {traceparent,tracestate,baggage} or a single opaque string) and explicit bounds in the core extensions spec.
Potential mapping: env var injection for subprocess + event facets (no known upstream flag).
Potential mapping: env var injection for subprocess + event facets (no known upstream flag).

items.title: Obs: tags / key-value annotations
notes: Allow callers to attach bounded tags (e.g., workflow ids, customer ids, repo ids) to every event and completion.
Prefer a small bounded string map {k: v} with strict limits on count and bytes.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would attach metadata; optional propagation via env.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would attach metadata; optional propagation via env.

items.title: Session: consolidate universal session controls
notes: Introduce `agent_api.session.*` as a first-class bucket for conversation/thread semantics (orthogonal to execution policy).
Update docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md rubric to add `agent_api.session.*`.
Decide whether to keep separate keys (resume/fork) or consolidate into a single session object under one extension key.
Session-related flags live on the headless --print surface: --continue, --resume, --session-id, --fork-session.
Session-related commands exist as non-interactive subcommands: codex exec resume, codex resume, codex fork.
